league.name: Premier League
league.description: England’s top division: 20 clubs, home-and-away season, three points for a win. It is known for pace, physical duels, and tight races at the top — a good first league to follow if you like constant drama and famous clubs.
league.styleOfPlay: Often end-to-end: quick transitions, pressing, and athletic midfield battles. Top teams mix that with modern positional play; smaller clubs may sit deeper and counter. Less “slow build-up only” than Spain; more sprint and duel intensity.
famousClubs: Manchester City — possession and titles in the 2010s–20s
Arsenal — youth, pressing, north London identity
Liverpool — Anfield atmosphere and European pedigree
Manchester United — global fanbase and historic trophy haul
Chelsea — London club with modern European success
famousPlayers: Erling Haaland — main striker at City
Bukayo Saka — Arsenal winger and England star
Mohamed Salah — Liverpool’s inverted right winger
Kevin De Bruyne — City playmaker and assist king
league.historySummary: The Premier League started in 1992 when top clubs broke away to share TV revenue. It replaced the old First Division branding and grew with global broadcasts. Traditions like Boxing Day fixtures and promotion/relegation keep every match meaningful.
rivalries: North London derby — Arsenal vs Tottenham
Liverpool vs Manchester United — historic national grudge
league.fanGuide: Learn the table: top four often means Champions League, bottom three means relegation. Saturdays remain the rhythm; midweek games pile up in winter. Chants, rivalry weeks, and manager pressure are part of the culture — pick one club and one derby to start.

teams.name: Manchester City
teams.shortHistory: For decades City were the “noisy neighbours” to United. Investment and smart coaching turned them into a Premier League powerhouse, with Pep Guardiola’s teams known for possession, control, and a first Champions League title in 2023.
teams.fanGuide: Sky blue is the colour to know. Supporters sing “Blue Moon” before kickoff at the Etihad. The club’s modern identity is slick football and trophies — less about one legendary era, more about sustained winning since the late 2000s.
legends: Sergio Agüero — 93rd-minute title winner in 2012; City’s greatest goalscorer
David Silva — tiny magician who ran attacks for a decade in sky blue
Vincent Kompany — captain who led the rebuild and scored huge derby goals
Yaya Touré — powerful midfielder; hero of two Premier League titles
currentKeyPlayers: Erling Haaland — centre-forward; main goal threat
Rodri — defensive midfielder; tempo and balance
Phil Foden — local academy star; links midfield and attack
Kevin De Bruyne — creator; crosses and through-balls from the right

teams.name: Arsenal
teams.shortHistory: Arsenal’s 2003–04 “Invincibles” went unbeaten in the league — still a benchmark in English football. After years outside the title race, Mikel Arteta rebuilt with homegrown talent and pressing football, pushing the club back toward the top.
teams.fanGuide: The Gunners wear red and white; the north London derby vs Tottenham is the fixture fans circle first. “North London Forever” plays after big wins at the Emirates. Long-time supporters still talk about the old Highbury ground and the clock on the stadium exterior.
legends: Thierry Henry — club record goalscorer; pace and composure defined the Wenger era
Dennis Bergkamp — non-scoring genius; first touch and link play
Tony Adams — “Mr Arsenal”; leader of the defence for 14 league titles
Patrick Vieira — tall, fierce midfielder; heart of the late 1990s teams
currentKeyPlayers: Bukayo Saka — right wing; goals, assists, and England star
Martin Ødegaard — captain; playmaker between the lines
William Saliba — centre-back; calm on the ball, strong in duels
Declan Rice — defensive midfield; tackles and progression

teams.name: Liverpool
teams.shortHistory: Liverpool built their reputation on European nights at Anfield and a fanbase that travels everywhere. Bill Shankly and Bob Paisley laid the trophy culture; the Klopp years added another Champions League and a first league title in 30 years in 2020.
teams.fanGuide: Red shirts, the Kop end, and “You’ll Never Walk Alone” before kickoff — that song is non-negotiable for newcomers. The Merseyside derby vs Everton is about city pride; matches vs Manchester United carry extra history. European games at Anfield are famous for loud, late comebacks.
legends: Steven Gerrard — one-club captain; Istanbul 2005 and famous long-range goals
Kenny Dalglish — player and manager; symbol of the club in the 1970s–80s
Ian Rush — record goalscorer; poacher in front of goal for years
Jamie Carragher — local defender; 737 appearances, now a vocal pundit
currentKeyPlayers: Mohamed Salah — right wing; cutting inside on his left foot
Virgil van Dijk — centre-back; organises the defensive line
Alisson Becker — goalkeeper; saves and calm distribution
Trent Alexander-Arnold — right-back; set pieces and crossing

teams.name: Chelsea
teams.shortHistory: Chelsea grew from a west London club into a global name through Abramovich-era investment and later American ownership, with repeated Premier League and Champions League success since the 2000s.
teams.fanGuide: Blue is the colour; “Carefree” and Shed End culture matter to supporters. The rivalry with Arsenal and Spurs frames the London football map.
legends: Frank Lampard — midfielder and club-record goals
Didier Drogba — big-game striker
John Terry — captain and defensive leader
currentKeyPlayers: Cole Palmer — attacking midfielder
Enzo Fernández — deep-lying playmaker

teams.name: Manchester United
teams.shortHistory: United built the Busby Babes story, Ferguson’s 26-year dynasty, and a global fanbase. Old Trafford and the 1999 treble remain reference points for English football history.
teams.fanGuide: Red shirts, Stretford End songs, and the Liverpool rivalry define the emotional calendar. “Glory Glory Man United” is the anthem newcomers hear first.
legends: Sir Bobby Charlton — Munich survivor and club icon
George Best — flair winger of the 1960s
Cristiano Ronaldo — two spells, goals and global fame
currentKeyPlayers: Bruno Fernandes — Portugal playmaker and captain
Marcus Rashford — academy forward

teams.name: Tottenham Hotspur
teams.shortHistory: Spurs were the first English club to win a major European trophy in the modern era (1963). The north London rivalry with Arsenal is the club’s heartbeat; recent decades mixed near-miss seasons with new stadium ambition.
teams.fanGuide: White shirts and navy trim; the north London derby is the fixture to learn first. “When the year ends in one, Spurs win something” is a famous supporter joke — know it before you repeat it.
legends: Jimmy Greaves — prolific goalscorer
Glenn Hoddle — elegant playmaker
Harry Kane — academy striker and club record scorer
currentKeyPlayers: Son Heung-min — forward and South Korea captain
James Maddison — creative midfielder

teams.name: Newcastle United
teams.shortHistory: Newcastle carry a passionate regional identity — the Tyne-Wear derby with Sunderland is historic. Modern investment aimed to restore trophy challenges after years of turbulence.
teams.fanGuide: Black and white stripes; Gallowgate End leads the noise. Supporters describe the club as more than football for the north east — learn the Tyne-Wear derby name even if fixtures are less frequent.
legends: Alan Shearer — local hero and Premier League record scorer
Peter Beardsley — technical forward
Kevin Keegan — player and beloved manager
currentKeyPlayers: Alexander Isak — Sweden striker
Bruno Guimarães — Brazil midfielder

teams.name: Aston Villa
teams.shortHistory: Villa are one of England’s founding clubs and European Cup winners in 1982. Birmingham’s claret and blue identity runs deep through the West Midlands.
teams.fanGuide: Claret and blue halves; Holte End is famous for volume. The Second City derby vs Birmingham City is the local grudge to know.
legends: Peter Withe — scorer of the 1982 European Cup final goal
Paul McGrath — elegant defender despite knee issues
Jack Grealish — local academy talent
currentKeyPlayers: Ollie Watkins — England striker
Morgan Rogers — attacking midfielder

teams.name: West Ham United
teams.shortHistory: East London club that has produced generations of England internationals from Geoff Hurst to Declan Rice. Known as the Hammers and famous for their academy's contribution to English football.
teams.fanGuide: West Ham fans are famously passionate. The club moved from Upton Park to the London Stadium in 2016 — the old ground is a touchstone of the identity debate.
legends: Bobby Moore
Geoff Hurst
Martin Peters

teams.name: Brighton & Hove Albion
teams.shortHistory: Brighton rose from the lower leagues to become a data-driven Premier League force under Graham Potter and Roberto De Zerbi. The Amex Stadium opened in 2011 and the club is known for developing and selling elite talent.
teams.fanGuide: The M23 derby against Crystal Palace is the rivalry that matters most. Brighton fans pride themselves on a progressive, modern club culture.

teams.name: Wolverhampton Wanderers
teams.shortHistory: One of the founding members of the Football League, Wolves won three First Division titles in the 1950s. Their modern resurgence under Nuno Espírito Santo brought Portuguese flair and Europa League football.
teams.fanGuide: The Black Country derby against West Brom is fierce. Molineux has one of the most distinctive atmospheres in the Midlands.
legends: Billy Wright
Steve Bull
Robbie Keane

teams.name: Crystal Palace
teams.shortHistory: South London club nicknamed the Eagles with one of the most atmospheric grounds in the Premier League. Known for the passionate Holmesdale Road end and a style built around wide attackers.
teams.fanGuide: The rivalry with Brighton — the M23 derby — defines their season. Selhurst Park's tight stands and vocal support create a unique home advantage.
legends: Ian Wright
Wilfried Zaha
Mark Bright

teams.name: Brentford
teams.shortHistory: West London club that reinvented itself as a data-analytics trailblazer. After 74 years outside the top flight, Brentford won promotion to the Premier League in 2021 and quickly established themselves with intelligent recruitment.
teams.fanGuide: The Bees are a community club that punches above their wage bill. Their approach to player recruitment is studied across the football world.

teams.name: Everton
teams.shortHistory: One of England's most storied clubs, Everton spent the first 130 years of their top-flight life at Goodison Park. Nine league titles and five FA Cups define a proud history. The Merseyside derby against Liverpool is the fiercest local derby in England.
teams.fanGuide: The Merseyside derby is everything. Goodison Park's unique atmosphere and the wall of noise from the bullpen end are unlike anything in English football.
legends: Dixie Dean
Alan Ball
Wayne Rooney

teams.name: Fulham
teams.shortHistory: West London club with one of football's most charming grounds in Craven Cottage, beside the Thames. Fulham are yo-yo club regulars between the Premier League and Championship, known for a technical style and attractive football.
teams.fanGuide: The Cottage's riverside setting is unique in English football. The West Stand's white facade and the neutral Putney End give it a village-football atmosphere even in the top flight.
legends: Johnny Haynes
George Best (briefly)
Clint Dempsey

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.quickFact: His meditation-style goal celebration became one of the Premier League’s most recognizable rituals.
players.playingStyle: Relentless finisher with explosive runs and ruthless positioning in the box.
quizHints: Rose through Molde and Salzburg before a prolific spell in Dortmund yellow.
His father Alf-Inge played in England for Nottingham Forest and Leeds.
Norwegian striker who wears sky blue and often scores with his head.

players.playingStyle: Calm pivot who shields the defense, recycles possession, and occasionally arrives late in the box.
quizHints: Spanish pivot who left Villarreal’s yellow submarine for Atlético briefly.
Scored in Istanbul to help his club complete a treble in 2023.
Often wears number 16 in sky blue — Euro 2024 winner with La Roja.
